content: Claude’s latest flagship models (Claude Opus 4.6/4.7/4.8, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Fable 5, Claude Mythos 5, and Claude Mythos Preview) support a 1M‑token context window; most other current Claude models (e.g., Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5) have a 200k‑token window. All request elements count toward this limit, including system prompts, full message history, tool definitions and results, images/PDFs (up to 600 pages/images for 1M‑context models, 100 for 200k), and all output tokens, including “extended thinking” tokens. Anthropic notes that as context length grows, “context rot” occurs—accuracy and recall degrade—so more context is not automatically better and careful curation, summarization, and compaction are important. To mitigate long‑context issues, they provide server‑side compaction (beta on several 1M‑context models), context awareness/task budgets, and recommend token counting and context editing to keep prompts within limits and maintain quality.
